reviews.review_text: Even better than its already brilliant predecessor, Hades 2 offers more content, a crisp combat system, lovable and exciting characters, and so many details that even after dozens of hours, you won't feel a bit bored. A masterful sequel that could hardly be any better.

I have almost 100 hours in this game. I got all of the achievements and it was WORTH IT!!!

This game isn’t just super accurate when it comes to Greek Mythology but it respects the player so much. It tells you what gifts characters want, which locations has the items you want, what exactly does the boons do etc. It doesn’t waste your time.

Gameplay wise it feels GREAT!! Supergiant Games know what they’re doing as always. I will be the first to admit that I was somewhat concerned when I saw they made a sequel because they’re known to make new IP over making a sequel. They proved they can make sequels. They kept what made Hades 1 great and made some adjustments. It’s a far better game!! (I still love Hades 1 btw)

The voice work is perfect. Every Voice Actor/Actress did a fantastic job. They were born to play these characters.   
Also, the soundtrack is very beautiful.

This is a 10/10 game. I highly recommend Hades 2.

[h1] A SPELLBINDING SEQUEL THAT ELEVATES THE MYTH [/h1]

[b][i]  Following up on a masterpiece is no easy task, but this sequel proves that the flame of the Underworld burns brighter than ever. Stepping into the boots of Melinoë brings a completely fresh, witchcraft-infused combat style that feels distinct, tactical, and incredibly fluid compared to Zagreus.

The addition of Omega moves, the dual-route progression between Chronos and the surface, and an entirely new roster of gods and boons make the gameplay loop addictively deep. Even without uncovering every single secret yet, the rich storytelling, stunning art direction, and stellar soundtrack make it a flawless and worthy successor to its legendary predecessor.

Death to Chronos. 9.5/10  [/i][/b]

Great game. I started with Hades so I'll use this review to compare Hades II to the original.

Gameplay:
Hades II has an edge. The weapons and aspects are an improvement, the Arcana Card system is flexible, and the wide variety of boons make a for ton of fun and viable builds.
Hades II also has a more carefully thought out reward system. It's possible to progress further in Hades II without as much of the relentless grinding that's needed in Hades.

Story:
Hades was superior to Hades II in this regard. Zagreus is a substantially more intriguing character than Melinoë. The central story of Hades is more touching, with a far better conclusion. While Hades II offers a broader cast, many of the additional characters add little to the experience. The epilogue was particularly lackluster.
A fantastic experience that manages to take everything great that the first game did and somehow make it even better

Gameplay is fine. If you like roguelites then this is the king. 

But if you want a good story look elsewhere. The original ending was done so poorly that due to the backlash Supergiant had to come back to the drawing board to level out their franchise better. 

My complaint comes from the writing. This game has the most inferior writing compared to the original. 

Every side character has very little character arcs and Arachne is the worst offender to all of them. We spend multiple runs [spoiler] building up a story of how she has changed and ready to ask for her old body back. [/spoiler] 
Arachne was the only character I wanted to see change. And I'm supposed to believe [spoiler] the goddess of WISDOM is petulant enough to not let go of a grudge. Are you serious? [/spoiler]. I'd expect that kind of arrogance from Zeus and Hera.

I dropped this game for months after the original request for the [spoiler] "nothing beats the power of family!" [/spoiler] ending and wish I had never picked it back up. I only bought this game in early access because of the proven story of Hades 1 and I will not make the same mistake again if Supergiant is this afraid to make their characters have interesting arcs. Telling stories where a character just doubles down is bad writing. Then the game continues this bad writing with Echo who chooses to remain a statue in a place of suffering. Like WTF?? How many different ways can you tell the same freaking story. 

This game does not deserve the overwhelming positive reviews that it has.

Hades was a great game, but Hades II blew my mind with its quality and detail. It has an interesting, expansive story and multiple paths to explore, as well as a huge variety of weapons, builds, and pets!
i started this game during a week where i had a closing shift every single every day bar one and rolled credits within seven days, might be the most captivated ive ever been by a game. 11/10

Phenomenal sequel. A pioneer of the roguelike genre and layers of complexity that maximizes delight. It is both a satisfying tactile experience, it is as well, a beautifully concise and eloquent story. You guys done it again. Can't wait for what's next.
Definitely an upgrade compared to Hades 1, story, items, boons etc wise, feels alot more balanced and actually more fun. Can reccomend, if enjoyed Hades 1 initially.

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Rating to the follow-up game of Hades

Hades II somehow pulls off the impossible: it takes everything that made the first game amazing and makes it bigger, deeper, and even more addictive.

The combat feels faster and more strategic, the build variety is insane, and every run gives you another reason to say, "just one more." Melinoë is a fantastic protagonist, bringing a different energy from Zagreus while still fitting perfectly into the world. The art, music, voice acting, and character writing are all top-tier, which is exactly what you'd expect from Supergiant Games.

What impressed me most is how polished the game feels. Even during Early Access, it launched with an enormous amount of content and was already being praised as one of the strongest Early Access releases in recent memory. Steam reviews remain overwhelmingly positive, with players praising the expanded gameplay systems, bosses, and progression.

Is it harder than the first game? Definitely. Some encounters can feel brutal, and a few mechanics might frustrate you at first. But every victory feels earned, and experimenting with different builds never gets old.

If you loved Hades, buying Hades II is a no-brainer. If you've never played either game, congratulations—you've just found the next 100+ hours of your life.

Pros:

Incredible combat and build variety
Amazing soundtrack and voice acting
Gorgeous art style
Tons of replayability
"Just one more run" syndrome is stronger than ever

Cons:

Sleep schedule may be permanently destroyed
Chronos doesn't care about your feelings

10/10 — The gods cooked again. 🔥
Great game, vastly exceeds the first one, absolute cinema.

The game improves on the first in almost every way. Melinoe's kit is both more variable, interesting and useable than Zag's, the boons are in general better, runs are shorter and the new way the game handles upgrades means you always go on expeditions with something in mind. You always have something to grind for, something to search for, and that leads you not even feeling it as the runs go by. 

The game's soundtrack is more varied in tones and instruments, and the charachters are frankly more charismatic at times (with the exception of the protag. Meg is very no-nonsese compared to Zag's roguish charm and sarcasm.)

All in all, if you've loved the first, you'll love the second even more. If you didn't play the first, Its an impecable action roguelite, and a testament to the heights indie gaming can reach.
As a lifeguard apprentice, this game taught me that swimming in sand is possible. I want to thank him because it has proven really useful in my life
Absolutely delightful game. Love every piece of it - the music, the dialog, visuals, graphics, game mechanics, the works. 10/10

Excellent game that built upon the original success and improved upon it while still keeping the core gameplay loop exciting and addicting as ever.

This is how a sequel should be done!

It's hard to say "not recommended" on a game that I've put 112 hours into. Early access was very fun and gameplay-wise I have little to complain about. It does many things better than the first Hades game, some worse, but is overall an improvement. If you only care for the gameplay, this game will surely be a blast, by all means, GLHF.

But I cannot stress how big of a letdown story-wise it has been.

Before launch, there were many things hinted at that would be expanded upon as the updates were being released. I was excited to learn what both the main story and the many interesting side quests had to offer, only to be thoroughly disappointed with almost every. single. one.

I was wondering how the main story was going to end, especially assuming the game would follow its predecessor in still allowing you to play after the ending. In my worst predictions, I could not have anticipated how rushed and badly written it actually turned out to be. It felt like a spit in the face and a waste of time spent on the game up until that point.

The vast majority of side quests also make you feel like you wasted your time on them. They might be really entertaining, or even exciting, to do, up until the last dialogue that makes it all just... not matter. Almost all of them end with the same thing: [spoiler] the character making you feel bad for trying to help [/spoiler].

The absolute worst example of this is [spoiler] waking up Hypnos [/spoiler]. Teased in I'm pretty sure every early access update since its first part, it seemed like something big was planned for it. There wasn't. It ends with [spoiler] a joke at the protag's expense and Hypnos just disappears forever. [/spoiler] Great. I love when developers make me feel excited and then purposefully don't deliver.

If you've been keeping on top of the quests until the epilogue, it leaves you wondering... what is the message of the game supposed to be? What's the moral of the story? [spoiler] That everybody can be redeemed (main quest) and we should preserve the status quo lest we disturb something or someone (side quests)? [/spoiler]

Terrible. Made everything feel bad in retrospect and makes the game feel unreplayable.

I LOVE Hades 2! I found the first game enjoyable enough, but everything that Hades did well, Hades 2 did even better. I've beaten the main story, but I haven't finished the epilogue yet. The gameplay loop is extremely engaging and it feels so rewarding when you beat a tough boss for the first time, or you finally have enough resources for a spell or weapon aspect you've been saving up for. The dialogue and character developments are great, and I'm glad that nothing is missable achievement wise (I want to 100% it eventually). 10/10, no comments.
The best rogue like I've played so far. It's ideal in every aspect. After 120 hours I don't feel bored at all. Beat it 100%. So much fun.

Very conflicted about this one. The gameplay, the music, the characters, the art, it's all absolutely stellar. But the story progression is painfully slow. Advancing some of the story only one interaction at a time per run, and it's still based on random encounters, and other text lines might take priority, it's just tedious. I'm well over 100 runs in, I've done almost all of the minor prophecies, but various questlines are barely halfway done, or haven't even triggered, despite talking to all characters at every opportunity.

I really enjoyed the first 40 hrs or so, after that I was just trying to wrap things up, and at this point I'll just stop. I triggered the true ending, and that's it. So, great game, lots of fun, but just be prepared that you're a slave to random encounters, and they may simply not trigger to advance every story line...

not gay enough. all the chemistry is with men all the women in the game have some big important relationship reason not to flirt with you. rookie writing mistake. make nemesis and melinoe girlfriends now. plot is just greek myth fanfic which is fine i guess. overall weirdly conservative tone to it tho. bastion had better writing than this. do better next time. gameplay, awesome, immaculate, incredibly fun and rewarding, improves on the first hades in many good ways

The game is good, but unlike others I would by no means say it's better than the first one. The game pretty strongly acknowledges, and addresses 2 problems the original game had, but I'd be lying if I said it was an improvement overall. 
In the first game you have to spam dash basically the entire time, the sequel fixes this by letting you hold for a sprint now which is good. The other thing it tries to address is repetition by adding a 2nd route to the game.
The 2nd route is where they imo failed, and the absolute worst part about it is this game was in early access for over a year with plenty of input; and they just refused to listen so we're forever stuck with X bad gimmicks and Z bugs. 
So as much as I like this game, I can't even recommend it, cause everytime I get to X boss I can't stand this single gimmick not at all cause I can't get past it, but because it's just annoying, it doesn't fit a fighting game, and I feel absolutely no satisfaction from winning it.
Perfect sequel to an amazing game. If you enjoyed the first game this really is more of the same, but very much MORE

It feels really horrible to give a Supergiant game a Not Recommended. Bastion, Transistor, Pyre, and Hades 1 are some of my favorite games of all time and I'd consider myself to be an outright Supergiant fanboy. But Hades II just feels redundant.
As you can see, I don't have much playtime at time of writing. The story is obviously great, the music is obviously great, the gameplay is obviously great, and yet it just feels like more of Hades 1. Which, objectively, is a good thing. Hades 1 was great. This game is great. And every time I go to boot this up, I find myself closing before I go through a single run. Why?
I think it might be because I've already played this game. I played Hades 1 in early access, played it on its release on the Epic store, and played it again when it came to steam. Now that Hades II is finally out of its early access, I feel like I'm entering my fourth playthrough, or even just DLC for Hades 1.
With each of Supergiant's games, I felt like they were pushing the envelope for the medium. Hell, Hades 1 redefined how amazing a rougelite could be. This really does nothing new. Sorry.

Rating 7/10

As much as it offers a lot of new gameplay mechanics that makes it super entertaining and never boring, the story makes it very silly with long useless conversations and shallow story that don't make any sense, 90% of character dialogues offer 0 extra info that relates to the story.
I'd still recommend the game for the fun gameplay it offered in return to the cheap price, it's not like I paid a AAAA game, devs are very appreciated for that and that what made me buy it without hesitation.
Spoiler ahead that involves game ending :
[spoiler]At the end it doesn't make any sense that the new good chronos is able and have much power than other realty chronos that he's able to manipulate their reality and kill them even while they're very powerless, don't know anything and can't feel anything !!
it's like the writer was cornered into finding an ending after watching Loki S2 (The series).[/spoiler]

Hades 2 builds on everything that made the first Hades great.

The best thing about the game is how fun it is to play and how even after completing the main part of the story there is still more to do. I've passed the 100 hour mark and have a couple of achievements to go.

The weapon aspects are all great and while some take a bit longer to get used to than others they all have their advantages. The addition of familiars is really good and adds another variation to play. Boons, Arcana and keepsakes all add variety to each run.

If I was to offer a criticism it is that the protagonist Melinoe is not as interesting as Zagreus was but that would be a very minor gripe.

Steam needs a mixed rating. It's a good game but strangely inferior to the first. You will need double the time to 100% it compared to the first, but there's not that much more content. I would like to see the epilogue, but I will die of boredom getting there. I already used every weapon and did the higher difficulty stuff, but there are still 30 hours of mindless grinding to do. Just doing the same run over and over again to get some dialog. Getting to the ending was great; getting to the epilogue ending the game falls apart. Then there's the endless dialog the game throws at you. Great at the start, but after a certain point it's just never-ending blabbering. It's the same stuff rephrased over and over again. They put way too much effort into stretching the ending to infinity. Being shorter would have helped the second half a lot. Steam achievements echo my opinion. All achievements after the first ending fall off a cliff. Only 26% finished the main story, which is not great, to be honest, and only 8% saw the epilogue.
it is a very fun game to just pickup do some runs and then just let it go it is my go to i got nothing to do game.

This game is something else. I have 34 hours and have had it for 3 days. I didnt mean to play that long it just happened. Probably the best artwork and design for a game I have ever seen. 10000% recommend as it  has incredible story and playstyle.

Somehow, Hades II improves on the original in every way. Better combat, more build variety, and a more rewarding progression system. It's also one of the few roguelikes that never feels repetitive, with each run offering something new.

I used to play in early access and had so much fun, but then kinda forgot about it. It's hard because each run takes ages, but totally worth it. The artstyle and design choices are to die for. 

Also ARTEMIS AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA:D I love her so so much!!! Make a new game with Artemis as a protagonist pls T_T
I could not get into it, unlike Hades 1, for a long time. I have finished  the game now and I am glad I perservered. What an amazing experience!

Probably the best roguelike game in existence and a step up from Hades I in every way. 

Would recommend 100% even if it's not on sale.
It's one of the most extensive games I've ever played. It sometimes feels impossible to finish because of the sheer amount of content it has, but in the end I loved it

Takes a bit longer to start going (mostly because your first weapon is kinda clunky) , but this game is definely better that the original. The new mechanics and items can seem a bit overwhelming, but in reality are mostly reskins from the previous game. The Arcana "upgrade"  system is much more interesting than the mirror of night, and rewards players for building around different weapons and boons. It also has a noticeable difficulty increase compared to Hades 1 (especially once the surface is unlocked). I personally found it much harder to just spam attack my way through the game, forcing me to learn the bosses and other enemies movesets and timings. 

Soundtrack is great as always, (I kinda miss the more metal themes of the first....)

Anyways: Hades 1 is great, if you liked it and actually read this, get Hades 2.

If you haven't played the or
Awesome game, Really cool interpretations of Greek Mythology, really want a 3 game to come out PLEASE!!!
A truly impressive experience and a prime example of how to develop a sequel to a game that was already great to begin with. Gameplay, animation, style, music, it stands out in every way.

Ok, 1 hour in and I can already say I love this game!! :zagwow:

This wasn't even a genre I enjoyed before playing Hades 1, but that game had me completely hooked to the screen and I ended up 100% completing it. I loved it so much that I didn't even think twice before buying Hades 2. It's simply wonderful and it looks absolutely gorgeous on the SD OLED. 

I usually wait for sales before buying games, but this time I couldn't even wait for the Summer Sale because I was too excited to play it. It's worth 100% of its price. I do miss Zag, though.

100% recommended. Incredible.

A true masterpiece sequel to a masterpiece orginal.
Having 100% completed all achievements in both Hades and Hades 2 ill try to make a comparison in this review (as non-ragebaitable as possible) of which game is the best out of the 2 (cuz both are definetely a worthy buy regardless) WARNING:SPOILERS AHEAD 
So:
Better Story: Hades ( i just prefer the whole climb to the surface thing of Zagreus rather than the family drama Melinoe goes through)
Better Gameplay: Hades 2 kept the fundamentals and roots of the original game and simply refined them in this one,not mad about it so my answer is Hades 2 (i can understand if someone would pick Hades though)
Better Graphics: Hades 2 definetely 
Better Artstyle and VFX: The artstyle has remained the same iconic one from the first game with a different witchy more moolight-y theme on it instead of the prince of the underworld,stygian theme the visuals and the effects of the mobs and bosses though are better in Hades 2 
Better Soundtracks: Hades 2 (Scylla and her band won me over with this one and also i particularly like the OST Mother of All Storms that plays out during the final boss fight at The summit that soundtrack is just so epic with its choir, Hades is not far behind with its OST's though ill go Hades 2 with this one
More Content: Hades 2 (you literally do what you did in Hades one going above to the surface but this time you can also go under plus chaos trials and you can  also enter the dream like realm of Hypnos so yeah definetely Hades 2)
Better Characters: As a main protagonist i like Zagreus more than Melinoe though Melinoe goes through more hardships during her journey as for the side characters its a same we can have builds of some speicific gods of Olympus like Athena,Artemis and Dionysus though they still remain helpful by situationally helping you throughout the game but not like standard gods that give you boons, the romance options are better since im still traumatized from getting whipped in Hades (im just not into that gang) all and all i feel like the side characters of Hades 2 help our protagonist more than Hades cuz they dont just stack him with side-quests but sometimes also provide with help in the stages you fight and also with resources so Hades 2 is my pick.
And now for the one that everyone's been waiting (well not really but cheers if you're still here)

Harder to Beat: Draw (Honestly the learning curve is the same having more content sure gives the edge to Hades 2 but i also have Hades in my heart even with its more limited straightforward journey compared to Hades 2 i havent beaten both games at max difficulty which is maxing  Heat/Fear having only 2 out of 3 Schelemeus statues in both games i never dared to challenge this game at its max difficulty since there was no achievement tied to it.The hardest challenge i beat in Hades was probably the 16 heat achivement and in Hades 2 the "Great Chaos Below" which is a random keepsake, enemies perk run with 20 fear in its gauge 

Anyway the point is both games are major masterpieces that defined the genre and whether you're buying this at full price or getting a sweet offer deal you're definetely gonna get your money's worth back in content.

10/10 major masterpiece just like its original 
Enjoy. Moonlight guide you.
very nice game, a little bit more complicated than the first one but as soon as you understand everything, it's really enjoyable and I can't stop recommending it

Been playing since the early access on and off. This game truly expands on the Hades, offer new style of gameplay. Especially love the cast system. Too many resources to gather though, which becomes useless later on
Fantastic story, art, and game mechanics.
I love how the game revolved around the dynamic between mortals and gods, and they also included a lot of quality of life changes that further improved the gameplay experience of the first game.

So good!!!!!! Just when I think I'm most of the way through the game, they give more content to work towards. The artwork is some of my fav. of all time, the music is to die for, and the writing is the cherry on top. The base game is challenging in a fun way. Just hard enough that I'm proud when I beat a new boss. Then they give you ways to up (and lower if you want/need) the difficulty. 10/10 possibly one of my top 3 games of all time.
Very fun game. Lots of depth to the characters and the story is well thought out and feels good to progress. Never really felt like there was any sort of grind. Moonlight guide us!
Fantastic rogueli* with satisfying difficulty and progression curves. Fun story, lots of interesting dialogue. Addictingly replayable.
Im not going  to say i fully understand everything, especially the dialog... its all greek to me.... but I like when you can flirt with people in the hot tub.
starts off a bit slow but progress is well gated and has kept me interested for over 50 hours.  I get diablo 2 level replayability vibes from this game

One of the greatest games of all time. Sets the bar for roguelikes to the height of Olympus.

Supergiant is the GOAT.
Is a great game, I just wish they had a DLC for zagreus side of the game. When mel sends him on the mission, would be nice to play from that perspective..

While this game is great in many respects and is worth the money to enjoy for a little while, I would describe this game overall as a disappointing flash in the pan when compared with its predecessor and would not recommend it to roguelike players looking for a challenging or deep experience. This game had the potential to be one of the greatest roguelikes of all time, and Supergiant fell miles short. 

The visuals, art, music, and gameplay are impeccable; however, there are a number of flaws in the roguelike design, mechanics and balancing that are noticeable almost from the very beginning but increasingly show themselves until they are impossible to ignore by hour 50 or so, when the arcana system of permanent upgrades are fully enhanced. 

The result is a fun to play, beautiful but uninteresting experience, with runs eventually feeling samey and balance issues often leaving one feeling railroaded into making certain choices because they are just better in every respect. All of this says nothing of the narrative, which is aggressively mediocre in my opinion with the exception of a couple of side characters. 

If you like Hades, "chill" roguelites, or pretty games, definitely check this out--just don't expect a huge payoff if you invest more time into it.

Hades II is a monument of the rougelike genre. The plot is good (though not quite the masterpiece of its predecessor), the music is excellent, and the core gameplay loop is one of the smoothest out there. I would highly recommend this to anyone who enjoyed the first game.
Amazing gameplay, amazing storyline, amazing characters, amazing amazing women
So much fun, basically just hades 1 but improved everything, every weapon feels equally as fun and balanced. I know I only have 20 hours but I feel like I could play this game for SO much longer
So happy with this game! and everything is Voiced!! its an amazing expirience to play 9.5/10!!!!
The best of its kind. The only downside is knowing that eventually I will run out of things to do.
Engaging story, consistent, challenging gameplay and a whole lot of hot people. 10/10
loved the addition of being able to go not only one direction to shake up a run's feel. this game only improved on the first games design. have loved every second of it/
it's really fun! There is a lot of interactions between melione and the olimpus gods, the majority of them are really interesting and fun.

I had already played Hades 1 before coming to this game. Having loved it very dearly, I was skeptical if No.  2 could top it. It certainly did. 
If you haven't played Hades 1, it isn't vital to the story, but you might miss a few references and feel slightly less immersed. 
It runs wonderfully on my MacBook, and I haven't found a bug yet.
The artwork is stunning and the variety of landscapes and colours are amazing.
Just like in the first game every character is fantastically charismatic and the voice acting is top notch, and every run feels rewarding as you constantly learn more about every character and build relationships.
I'm almost 500 hours in and I still haven't finished a couple of the sub-plots.
The combat is masterful and the new weapons and aspects are so cool (except for those useless torches).
The boss fights are great and, like the whole game, are perfectly balanced between challenging enough to feel rewarding, but not so hard that you'll be stuck on anyone for a really long time.
The game has essentially infinite re-playability thanks to the two paths, hundreds of boons and the fear system to make the game as hard as you want (equivalent to heat in the first game).
Honestly a must play for rogue like fans, or just anyone honestly, you'll have an absolute blast and remember to pause the game while fighting Chronos.

My babe Judy Alice Melinoe Lee is the hottest video game female character ever. Have you seen them thighs on her 3d model?
But tbh, there's something in this game that is even hotter.. and that's the gameplay
Also, long live Sea Star

Slightly prefer the story in the first game, but this game's is phenomenal too. Music still great as expected. Art is VERY pretty, couldn't think of how they could improve upon the first game's art style but they did. Combat extremely fun. Feels better than the first one because all the weapons and aspects feel more unique and viable. Even the ones with weird mechanics are extremely fun! Don't think I've ever ran into bugs. I got a potato PC and it still runs smooth.  Overall, definitely a must play even if you haven't played the first one. 

MAKE ARACHNE ROMANCEABLE. Also make a third one, PLEASE.

Hades II checks all boxes of a crossover between art and entertainment. Everything in the game is excellent. Even the story arcs were fixed after the fanbase feedback, and which it makes me respect Supergiant Games even more. 

The name makes us think that this is simply a sequel of another excellent game, the first Hades. In fact it combines very well-known elements of the first game: the previously developed story, the isometric setting, the commands, the weapons system, the roguelike progression, to mention a few. On the top of all of it, Supergiant team added more features that don't take the player too much time to figure how they work. Of course we miss the double dash, for instance, but the perks don't need to be identical. The dialogues are very sharp, enriched with Greek mythology, and the personality traits of the gods, the titans, and all other characters. Even the romantic paths leave a good amount of substance for the imagination. 

This is a must-have game. I see it as historically significant, and it should be a case study for everyone that wants to make video games.

In many ways a huge improvement over the original, in some ways not.  Though catching lightning in a bottle twice may be unreasonable supergiant has been well known for their consistent high quality games and Hades 2 is no exception.  Everything from the gameplay, art, and music departments are just as good if not better than the original.  The extra content also offers alot more room for variety per run.  That said one of the things that made Hades 1 exceptional was its top notch character writing and a tight scoped story that brought all of its best features together.  

Hades 2 is much more ambitious in both of these regards but the greater stakes of the conflict create dissonance with both the constant cycle of the runs as well as the relationships with the other characters(who I also just think arn't as endearing or multifaceted as Hades 1s cast) to the point where some awkward revelations and developments towards the end lack the punch that matches how fittingly intense the gameplay is.  

The above complaint is only to say that Hades 2 does not completely surpass the first but in many ways it does and that should not be forgotten.  This is one of the strongest action games of the decade and will easily carry you from minute one to the credits. .
this is a nice game! when im playing other games 1-2hours my back hurts. but this game i play 6-8hrs no back pain hahaha. nice game!
Beautifully written, addicting gameplay, truly one of the best indie games I've ever played. 10/10

Game is amazing and addicting! Not sure if it hits the same as the original Hades, but the movement, attacks, and abilities feel amazing. Also the boon collection and every other system is just incredible.

9.5/10
Roughly double the content of the original, and gameplay close enough to feel familiar but different enough to feel new. This applies to characters and their designs as well.
Story lacks some of the punch of the first, but it is still several steps above all but the best narrative-driven  AAA titles.
The mid-game difficult spike is also very real here, so be prepared.
If you loved Hades I'd wager you'll love this too.

I have 270 hours of play and i STILL WANT MORE. IT's so fun to experiment with the weapons and pets as well as just play the game! My favorite fight is still with Scylla as the music is equally excellent! Well worth my time and money!
If you loved Hades, you'll love this. It starts feeling very similar in terms of gameplay but soon expands into new areas, and the story is just as engaging as its predecessor if not more so.
Amazing gameplay, great story, excellent all around

One of the best games I have played recently, I have played the game for over 30 hrs and not planning to stop soon, took me around 48 runs to get the first victory over Chronos but it really felt so good going back to defeat him again and again and to progress through the amazing underworld 

Hades 2 is simply one of the best gaming universes I have been to, to the point I sometimes just park my car, get my handheld out and start diving into this world again.

I have never been a fan of 2d games but this rogue experience for me is the most definitive ones

Fantastic game! I do feel like the boon options were much better in the first Hades and the arcana system is a bit over complicated compared to just spending darkness at the mirror. I originally played Hades on console and was worried the keyboard & mouse controls for PC would make the game less enjoyable but I actually now prefer playing on PC over console.
I absolutely love this game! The story is incredible so far and despite having played for quite some time I haven't found myself getting frustrated or bored of the gameplay loop.

An upgrade from Hades 1. Combat is fun and so is the story up until defeating Chronos, then it gets a bit repetative.
Also works on Linux

First let me say I bought this game for full price which is something I would almost never do and yet I still think its worth the money. This is one of the most polished and beautiful games I have ever played, I haven't played HADES 1 and this is also the first roguelite I have played but the combination of story and hardcore roguelike gameplay is extremely enjoyable. would recommend to anyone who thinks they may enjoy the genre

i love this game so much, the lore made me cry, the art left me speechless, the music was stuck im my head for months.
love the universe, love the fighting system(i suck at fighting, dosent mean the system is bad)

If you played the first Hades (or Bastion/Transistor), Hades II is an improvement on the same formula. Action roguelike with dating-sim elements. 
I've played all of Supergiant's games but this was the first I felt the need to 100%. The core gameplay loop is very satisfying and the dialogue is worth seeking out. 
It took me ~40hrs to complete the main story and another 55 to 100%. The difficulty is adjustable, both up and down. Much like Monster Hunter's weapons, each weapon is viable but one may click for you over the others (for me it was the Axe).
Bought the game on sale but would not have regretted buying at full price. Looking forward to the next Supergiant game!

I was going to write this review after like 10 hours of gameplay but thought I would wait until I have experienced more of it to be sure Im giving it the proper credit it deserves.

 55 hours in now and having finished the main storyline I can safely say that this game is AMAZING, if you have played and liked the first game then I think you will absolutely fall in love with this one and if you haven't played the prequel then I highly recommend trying at least this one if not both of them. 

The art and setting in this game is absolutely STUNNING I loved it since the first time I loaded in and it kept surprising me along the way even after 50 hours of playtime, coming back the main hub is such a pleasant sight every time, especially as you can customize it to your liking and the options are so vast, you are more than likely to find something you love.

 The characters I cannot give enough credit, individually they are really interesting each with their own different personality that fits the character they are depicting so well but also their dynamics with the main character are all SO good, the writers really did an incredible job.

The story is very touching, HUGE credits to the voice actors. If you have played the first game it will probably have a bigger impact on you, however it is still a very good standalone story that you dont need to have played the first game to understand. (Mild spoilers) it follows the protagonist, Melinoe, who is the daughter of Hades in her quest to defeat the Titan of time, Chronos. Who captured her family and took hold of the underworld.  would love to dive more into this but I dont want to spoil any new players who might read this.

In terms of gameplay I would say its a big improvement from the first game but that might just be a personal prefference. This game compared to the previous gives you the ability to run after a dash which feels really nice to use to dodge enemies and attacks and I much preffer this mechanic compared to the former as it kinda felt like dash simulator. In terms of Olympian boons they are a lot more distinct in this game, allowing for many different strong combos for each weapon which speaking of, it also introduces a new mechanic with the omega moves which use magick to perform, a sort of mana system for those moves which are very strong thus requiring you to use magick and lock yourself into a short cast time to perform them, this mechanic adds a vast new array of moves and I absolutely love it, you can choose to focus on your omega moves or ignore them entirely or even use a combination of both regular moves and omega moves depending on your playstyle and weapon.

This game completely blew away my expectations and the few tiny complaints that I may have about it are absolutely laughable in comparison to everything this game does incredibly well, it is a must try in my opinion.

i didn't even notice it came to full release, and as soon as i noticed i couldn't stop playing for as much of my day i could spare until i completed the main story. looking forward to continuing everyone's storylines and finishing the game, i'm certain it will be just as satisfying as the first
Its a blast, combat is far more versatile then the first game, and feels smoother overall. However, the story is pretty poor, so if you want to play just for that I wouldn't recommend
Game is rad. Bless the devs for not including an achievement for an all modifier run.

If you're a fan of the first game you'll probably love this one too. The combat in the first Hades is a little faster, but the combat system in the sequel is a bit more fleshed out. Music is peak, no notes.

I played Hades 1 all the way through, 100h+, but this one just isn't very very good. The story is wonky and boring. The gameplay just isn't as good. They start you out with an underpowered attack you get to spam against uninteresting foes.

9/10 

Supergiant is just really good at making games. 

I'm really glad they chose a sequel to *Hades* as their next project, because there was so much meat left on that bone. This is bigger, better, and more ambitious than Hades 1. 

Melinoe is such a different character to Zagreus, but you fall in love with her all the same. I think the *plot* for the first game is SLIGHTLY better (because it's simpler), but this is still absolutely worth your time.
Όσο αγορίστικο(derogatory) ήταν το Hades 1 τοσο κοριτσίστικο(complimentary) είναι το Hades 2. Great game, makes me think of my girlfriends all of the time, they also have great mechanics
Here, on this night, shall you enjoy this amazingly extraordinary game to its fullest potential, So Mote it Be.
If you like Hades, you'll like this game too. I'd say it's better than Hades, but only because they iterated super well

I loved the original Hades and wasn't sure anything could top it. Hades II proved me wrong.

The combat is sharper, the build variety is massive, and the story gives Melinoë her own identity rather than just being a Zagreus reskin. The soundtrack and art direction are some of Supergiant's best work yet.

60 hours in and every run still feels fresh. An easy masterpiece... highly recommended

Incredible music, fun and varied gameplay, the characters and the sheer amout of lines they have are so charming and well done. 

I am in fact biased because I loved the first so much that I named my cat Zagreus.
strong lady called me stud, and theres good game play too but thats not as importent then the pretty people.

The gameplay is more complex, allowing for even more builds than Hades 1. The two routes makes the game even more replayable, and they give Darren Korb a chance to flex his musical prowess by using so many different musical styles and influences in his compositions. It's a satisfying development from Hades 1, retaining many of the core mechanics and themes of the original while introducing many new elements that give the game its own vibe.
Amazing game, keep coming back for me, one of the best single player games in a while!

Truly a masterpiece once again. I think the story is slightly weaker (may be biased as I played before they changed the ending slightly) but the gameplay is improved from the first game so it makes up for it. Easy 9.5/10
Really good follow up to the first one. Only thing i can say is the weapons arent as good, and you play as a woman so its completely unrealistic. A+

Hades & Hades II are fun to play game with a nice storyline. With boons and others power-ups being collected along the way each run-through is different. Hades II is a nice follow-up with lots of new things that makes the gameplay feel familiar and still completely fresh.

Great game overall.
Had to cringe during a few dialogues and I'm not a big fan of the witch theme but the actual gameplay is very good.
Also, give us good Pan daggers

Hades II is not just “more Hades.” It is Hades after drinking darker magic and becoming more dangerous.

The combat feels deeper, the builds are more layered, the atmosphere is moodier, and Melinoë gives the game a completely different identity from Zagreus. It still has the Supergiant magic: gorgeous art, amazing music, sharp writing, and that dangerous “one more run” addiction.

It is bigger, more ambitious, and somehow still feels handcrafted instead of bloated.

Hades was lightning in a bottle.  
Hades II proves Supergiant knew exactly how to catch it again.

10/10 — witchcraft, trauma, and another ruined sleep schedule.

I can't stop playing. This game is so good. My only gripes are that it takes a long time until you feel like an absolute beast like in the original. The resource gathering is a little annoying because I just want to play the game, but that's just me. Anyways, good game. I recommend
